rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes; avoid introducing package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees using visible ports and constants, not implementation-specific subcomponents.
Implementation-level AGREE bindings must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE annexes should only include expressions and declarations that are valid within the AADL scope of the target component.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees in component types should refer to component ports, not subcomponent paths.
Assign statements in implementation scope must bind to existing output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
